ExtraDescr.brief: A class representing an additional descriptive entry associated with game objects or rooms.
ExtraDescr.details: The ExtraDescr class provides a means to attach supplementary descriptive information to game objects or environments, enhancing the interactive experience. Each instance contains a keyword that identifies the description during look or examine commands, and a description string that offers detailed information. The class supports multiple constructors for default initialization, initialization with specific keyword and description, and copying from existing instances. It maintains a linked list structure through the 'next' pointer, allowing multiple extra descriptions to be chained together for complex descriptions. The class includes a virtual destructor to ensure proper cleanup in inheritance hierarchies, and disables assignment operator to prevent unintended copying. Its design enables flexible and efficient management of descriptive data, facilitating dynamic and context-sensitive interactions within the game system.
ExtraDescr.notes: The class employs a linked list structure for chaining multiple extra descriptions, enabling efficient management of variable-length descriptive data. The use of a virtual destructor ensures proper cleanup in inheritance scenarios, and copying is handled via the copy constructor to maintain data integrity.
ExtraDescr.rationale: ExtraDescr exists to provide a flexible mechanism for associating additional descriptive text with game entities, enriching the player's experience. It fits into the system architecture as a component that supports dynamic, context-sensitive descriptions, which are essential for immersive gameplay and detailed object or environment representations.
ExtraDescr.name: ExtraDescr

usages.class_extra_descr, ExtraDescr::ExtraDesc: Used to initialize a new ExtraDescr object by copying the 'keyword' and 'description' from an existing ExtraDescr instance within the copy constructor.
usages.class_room, const ExtraDescr * Room::ext: Referenced as the return type of the function, indicating the function returns a pointer to an ExtraDescr object.
usages.handler_8cc, const ExtraDescr * get_extr: Used as the initial pointer in the loop to traverse linked ExtraDescr objects.

next.brief: Pointer to the next ExtraDescr node in the linked list.
next.details: This variable references the subsequent node in a singly linked list of ExtraDescr objects, which store additional descriptive information for game objects or rooms. It is initialized to nullptr when a new ExtraDescr node is created, indicating the end of the list. The linked list structure enables dynamic traversal, insertion, and removal of extra descriptions, supporting flexible management of supplementary descriptive data within the game environment. It is commonly used in functions that iterate through or modify the list of extra descriptions associated with an entity.
next.notes: The linked list pattern allows efficient insertion and deletion of descriptive entries without reallocating memory for the entire list. Proper handling of this pointer is essential to maintain list integrity during modifications.
next.rationale: This pointer facilitates the organization of multiple descriptive entries in a sequential manner, enabling the game to efficiently manage and display additional information for objects and rooms as needed.
next.name: next

usages.area__handler_8cc, void clone_object(Obj: Used as the iterator in the loop to traverse the linked list of extra descriptions in the parent object.

usages.act__obj_8cc, void do_engrave(Character: Traverses linked lists of ExtraDescr objects to find or add engraving descriptions.
usages.wiz__gen_8cc, void do_owner(Character *c: Used as a temporary pointer to traverse linked lists of ExtraDescr objects.
usages.class_room_prototype, RoomPrototype::~Ro: Used as the pointer to traverse and delete each node in the linked list of extra descriptions.

keyword.brief: Stores the keyword associated with an extra description for look or examine commands.
keyword.details: The `keyword` member contains the specific term used to identify or reference an extra description within the game, primarily during look or examine actions. It is initialized with the relevant term and stored as a `String`, a custom string class that provides case-insensitive comparisons and advanced manipulation capabilities. This variable enables efficient matching of user input to the corresponding description, supporting flexible and user-friendly interactions.
keyword.notes: Since `keyword` is stored as a `String`, it benefits from case-insensitive comparisons, allowing for more forgiving command matching. Proper initialization ensures accurate identification during gameplay.
keyword.rationale: This variable exists to associate a specific term with an extra description, facilitating quick and reliable look or examine interactions by matching user input to the correct description in the game environment.
keyword.name: keyword

description.brief: A string that stores the descriptive text associated with an object or feature.
description.details: The 'description' variable contains the textual description that provides additional details, visual cues, or markings for an object or feature within the game. It is initialized and modified through functions such as 'do_engrave', which allows for adding, updating, or removing descriptive text, and is accessed during player commands like 'look' to display specific information. The use of the String class enables advanced string manipulation, including case-insensitive comparisons and other features, facilitating flexible and efficient handling of descriptive content. This variable is essential for conveying visual details and enhancing the immersive experience by presenting relevant descriptive information to players.
description.notes: The description is stored using a custom String class that supports enhanced manipulation, which is useful for dynamic updates and searches within the game environment.
description.rationale: This variable exists to maintain and provide access to descriptive details of objects, supporting gameplay interactions that involve examining or modifying object appearances and features.
description.name: description

ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr().brief: Constructs an ExtraDescr object with default-initialized members.
ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr().details: This default constructor creates a new instance of the ExtraDescr class without performing any custom initialization. It relies on the compiler-generated default initialization for all member variables, ensuring that the object starts in a valid default state. No additional logic, resource allocation, or exception handling occurs during construction.
ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr().throws: This constructor does not throw exceptions.
ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr().notes: Since this constructor performs default initialization, it is suitable when the member variables have appropriate default constructors or default values. Users should ensure that the object is properly configured after construction if necessary.
ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr().rationale: This constructor is used when creating an ExtraDescr object with default values, often as a placeholder or before setting specific attributes in subsequent code.
ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr().name: ExtraDescr

ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr(const String &k,.brief: Constructs an ExtraDescr object by initializing its keyword and description members.
ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr(const String &k,.details: This constructor initializes an ExtraDescr instance by directly assigning the provided keyword and description strings to its internal member variables. The String class used supports advanced features such as case-insensitive comparisons, which can facilitate matching user input with the stored keyword during gameplay interactions. This setup ensures that each ExtraDescr object accurately associates a specific keyword with its descriptive text, enabling efficient lookups and examination commands within the game environment.

params.k: The keyword that identifies the extra description, used as an identifier.
params.d: The description text providing supplementary information related to the keyword.

ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr(const String &k,.returns: This is a constructor; it does not return a value.
ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr(const String &k,.notes: The use of the String class allows for flexible and case-insensitive matching of keywords, improving user interaction reliability.
ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr(const String &k,.rationale: This constructor is used whenever a new extra description needs to be associated with a specific keyword, such as when creating or loading game objects with additional descriptive details for examine or look commands.
ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr(const String &k,.name: ExtraDescr

ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr(const ExtraDescr.brief: Constructs a new ExtraDescr object as a copy of an existing one.
ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr(const ExtraDescr.details: This copy constructor creates a new ExtraDescr instance by duplicating the keyword and description fields from the provided object. It performs a straightforward member-wise copy, ensuring that the new object retains the same descriptive data as the original. This facilitates safe duplication of extra description entries, which can be useful when managing multiple objects or rooms with similar descriptions, without sharing references that could lead to unintended modifications.

params.e: The existing ExtraDescr object to copy from.

ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr(const ExtraDescr.returns: This constructor does not return a value.
ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr(const ExtraDescr.notes: This constructor performs a shallow copy of the string members, which is sufficient given that std::string manages its own memory. It does not perform deep copying beyond member-wise duplication.
ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr(const ExtraDescr.rationale: This copy constructor is used when duplicating extra description entries, such as when copying game objects or room descriptions to ensure each instance maintains its own data without shared references, supporting safe modifications and management of descriptive data.
ExtraDescr::ExtraDescr(const ExtraDescr.name: ExtraDescr

virtual ExtraDescr::~ExtraDescr().brief: Virtual destructor for the ExtraDescr class to ensure proper cleanup of resources in inheritance hierarchies.
virtual ExtraDescr::~ExtraDescr().details: This destructor is declared as virtual to ensure that when an object of a derived class is deleted through a pointer to the base class, the derived class's destructor is called correctly. Although the destructor body is empty in this implementation, declaring it virtual guarantees proper resource deallocation for any subclasses that may override cleanup procedures. This design facilitates safe inheritance and polymorphic behavior, preventing resource leaks and undefined behavior during object destruction.
virtual ExtraDescr::~ExtraDescr().returns: This function does not return a value.
virtual ExtraDescr::~ExtraDescr().notes: The destructor is intentionally empty, relying on derived classes to implement any specific cleanup if necessary. Declaring it virtual is crucial for correct polymorphic destruction in inheritance hierarchies.
virtual ExtraDescr::~ExtraDescr().rationale: This destructor is used when deleting objects through base class pointers to ensure that all resources are properly released, especially in systems where ExtraDescr may be subclassed or extended.
virtual ExtraDescr::~ExtraDescr().name: ~ExtraDescr

ExtraDescr & ExtraDescr::operator=(const.brief: Assigns the values from another ExtraDescr object to this object.
ExtraDescr & ExtraDescr::operator=(const.details: This copy assignment operator allows one ExtraDescr object to be assigned the values of another, effectively copying the state of the source object into the destination object. It ensures that all relevant data members are copied correctly, maintaining the integrity and consistency of the object.

params.rhs: The source ExtraDescr object from which values are to be copied.

ExtraDescr & ExtraDescr::operator=(const.returns: A reference to the current object after assignment, allowing for chained assignments.
